### Retrieve

`<dynamics:retrieve>`

Retrieves an entity.

- Retrieve an Entity - `https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/mt607871.aspx`

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Logical Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Logical name of the entity. It is always the schema name in lower case.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the entity to retrieve.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Retrieve Multiple

`<dynamics:retrieve-multiple>`

Retrieve multiple by URL.

- Query Data using the Web API - `https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/gg334767.aspx`

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Data Query URL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL to use for data retrieval.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Page Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of items in a results page.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>5000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>Non Repeatable Iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- DYNAMICS:OPERATION_FAILED

### Retrieve Multiple By Query

`<dynamics:retrieve-multiple-by-query>`

Retrieve multiple by DSQL query.

- Query Data using the Web API - `https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/gg334767.aspx`

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Datasense Query</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>DSQL query used to retrieve. The query is transformed in a retrieve URL internally.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Page Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of items in a results page.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>5000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expand</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Information about the specified relationship to include in the response.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>Non Repeatable Iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- DYNAMICS:INVALID_PAGE_SIZE

- DYNAMICS:OPERATION_FAILED

- DYNAMICS:UNAUTHORIZED

### Unauthorize

`<dynamics:unauthorize>`

Deletes all the access token information of a given resource owner ID so that it is impossible to
execute any operation for that user without doing the authorization dance again.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Resource Owner Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the resource owner for whom to invalidate access.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#dynamics-config)

### Update

`<dynamics:update>`

Updates an entity.

- Create New Entity - `https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/gg328090.aspx`
- Associate Entity Upon Creation - `https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/mt607875.aspx#Anchor_4`
- Batch Request - `https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/mt607719.aspx`

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Logical Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Logical name of the entity. It is always the schema name in lower case.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Attributes of the entity.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Body encoding.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>If no value is specified, set the Mule default encoding.</p></li><li><p>If a value is specified, set the <code>-M-Dmule.encoding</code> VM argument to the same value to avoid an encoding mismatch.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#dynamics-config)

#### Throws

- DYNAMICS:CONNECTIVITY
- DYNAMICS:INVALID_PAGE_SIZE
- DYNAMICS:NOT_FOUND
- DYNAMICS:OPERATION_FAILED
- DYNAMICS:PARSE_ERROR
- DYNAMICS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DYNAMICS:TIMEOUT
- DYNAMICS:UNAUTHORIZED
- DYNAMICS:UNKNOWN

### Update Multiple

`<dynamics:update-multiple>`

Updates multiple entities.

- Update Entity - `https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/mt607664.aspx`
- Associate Entities Upon Update - `https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/mt607875.aspx#Anchor_4`
- Batch Request - `https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/mt607719.aspx`

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Logical Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Logical name of the entity. It is always the schema name in lower case.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Entities Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of the entities' attributes to create as a list of maps.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Use Single Transaction</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Use a single transaction for an update. If the update fails, the transaction is rolled back.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Continue On Error</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Continue in case of error. The default value of <code>false</code> stops updating records after one update fails.</p></div><div><p>You can use <strong>Continue On Error</strong> in conjunction with <strong>Use Single Transaction</strong>. Suppose you want to execute an operation with a payload of 10 records. This payload contains one broken record, number 5, that fails. The other 9 records succeed. The following table shows the output, which depends on the values of <strong>Continue On Error</strong> and <strong>Use Single Transaction</strong>:</p></div><table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Use Single Transaction</th><th>Continue on Error</th><th>Expected result</th><th>Successful records</th><th>Failed records</th><th>Unprocessed records</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>False</p></td><td><p>False</p></td><td><p>No rollback. Stops processing records after a failed record.</p></td><td><p>1-4</p></td><td><p>5</p></td><td><p>6-10</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>False</p></td><td><p>True</p></td><td><p>No rollback. Continues to process records even after a failed record.</p></td><td><p>1-4, 6-10</p></td><td><p>5</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>True</p></td><td><p>False</p></td><td><p>If the connector fails to update an entity, no records are committed.</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td><td><p>Failed records 1-10</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>True</p></td><td><p>True</p></td><td><p>If the connector fails to update an entity, no records are committed.</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td><td><p>Failed records 1-10</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td></tr></tbody></table></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Body encoding.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>If no value is specified, set the Mule default encoding.</p></li><li><p>If a value is specified, set the <code>-M-Dmule.encoding</code> VM argument to the same value to avoid an encoding mismatch.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#BulkOperationResult">Bulk Operation Result</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#dynamics-config)

#### Throws

- DYNAMICS:CONNECTIVITY
- DYNAMICS:INVALID_PAGE_SIZE
- DYNAMICS:NOT_FOUND
- DYNAMICS:OPERATION_FAILED
- DYNAMICS:PARSE_ERROR
- DYNAMICS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DYNAMICS:TIMEOUT
- DYNAMICS:UNAUTHORIZED
- DYNAMICS:UNKNOWN

## Object Types

- [Bulk Item](#BulkItem)
- [Bulk Operation Result](#BulkOperationResult)
- [Expiration Policy](#ExpirationPolicy)
- [Reconnect](#reconnect)
- [Reconnect Forever](#reconnect-forever)
- [Reconnection](#Reconnection)
- [Redelivery Policy](#RedeliveryPolicy)
- [Repeatable File Store Iterable](#repeatable-file-store-iterable)
- [Repeatable In Memory Iterable](#repeatable-in-memory-iterable)

### Bulk Item

Information about a bulk item.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Exception</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Exception if the item fails.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Item ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Message to add context to this item, such as an error description, a warning, or information related to the operation.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Payload</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Actual data the entity represents.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Status Code</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional status code.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Successful</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether or not the request is successful.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Bulk Operation Result

Information about a bulk operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Operation ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Items</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#BulkItem">Bulk Item</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Ordered list of bulk items.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Successful</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether or not the operation is successful. Must be <code>true</code> if and only if all the child DynamicsBulkItem entities are also successful.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

Configures an expiration policy strategy.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the maximum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

Configures a standard reconnection strategy, which specifies how often to reconnect and how many
reconnection attempts the connector source or operation can make.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts the Mule app can make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to
reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if, when an app is deployed, a connectivity test does not pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Allow the deployment to fail.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Ignore the results of the connectivity test.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a
redelivery policy to any source in a flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of times that a redelivered request can be processed unsuccessfully before returning a <code>REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED</code> error.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Use Secure Hash</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule uses a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secure hashing algorithm to use if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>true</code>. If the payload of the message is a Java object, Mule ignores this value and returns the value that the payloadâs <code>hashCode()</code> returned.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more expressions that determine if a message is redelivered. This property can be set only if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the object store that stores the redelivery counter for each message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Iterable

Repeatable file store iterable type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of instances to keep in memory. If more than that is required, content on the disk is buffered.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>Max In Memory Size</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Iterable

Repeatable in memory iterable type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount of instances to initially keep in memory to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can fit into this buffer, then the buffer expands according to the <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong> attribute, with an upper limit of <strong>Max In Memory Size</strong>. Default value is <code>100</code> instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of <code>0</code> or lower means that the buffer must not expand, meaning that a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised when the buffer gets full. Default value is <code>100</code> instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used, a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised. A value lower than or equal to <code>0</code> means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Microsoft Dynamics 365 Connector 2.6

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure a source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-input-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems, as described in
[View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[Microsoft Dynamics Reference](microsoft-dynamics-365-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add Microsoft Dynamics 365 Connector to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code
with the connectorâs namespace and schema location and to add the required dependencies to the
projectâs `pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In the **Add Dependencies to Project** window, type `dynamics 365` in the search field.
3.  Click **Microsoft Dynamics 365** in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of these sources
to use with Microsoft Dynamics 365 Connector:

- **On new object**  
  starts your app when an object is created
- **On modified object**  
  Starts your app when an object is modified
- **On deleted object**  
  Starts your app when an object is deleted
- **HTTP > Listener**  
  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port
- **Scheduler**  
  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an **On New Object** source, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Dynamics 365 > On new object**.

    ![On New Object source selected in Mule Palette view](_images/dynamics-365-select-on-new-object.png)

2.  Drag **On new object** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **On new object** **General** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the
    **Display Name** field.
4.  Specify a value for the **Entity type** field, for example `account`.  
    The entity type string describes business data that is defined in the Microsoft Dynamics 365
    instance.

    ![General configuration fields for On New Object source](_images/dynamics-365-on-new-object-general.png)

5.  Click the plus sign (+) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of the **On new object** source in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, specify the connection information for the connector.
7.  On the **TLS** tab, optionally specify the TLS information for the connector.
8.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
10. Click **OK**.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you immediately define a specific operation for
that connector to perform.

To add an operation for Microsoft Dynamics 365 Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Dynamics 365** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas and to the right of the input source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, itâs best to configure a global element that all instances of that
connector in the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the
authentication credentials that the connector requires to access the target Microsoft Dynamics 365
system. Microsoft Dynamics 365 Connector supports the OAuth connection, OAuth client credentials,
and OAuth username and password authentication types.

To configure the global element for Microsoft Dynamics 365 Connector, follow these steps:

1.  Select the operation in the Studio canvas.
2.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (+) next to the **Connector
    configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
3.  On the **General** tab, in **Connection**, select the authentication method to configure:
    - [Connection (OAuth 2.0 authentication)](#oauth-connection)
    - [OAuth 2.0 client credentials authentication](#oauth-client-credentials-connection)
    - [OAuth 2.0 username and password authentication](#oauth-username-password)

4.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
5.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
6.  Click **OK**.

### OAuth 2.0 Authentication

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

OAuth 2.0 delegates user authentication to the service hosting the user account.

To configure OAuth 2.0 authentication:

1.  Enter the following required information on the **General** tab of the **Global Element
    Properties** screen:
    - **Resource**  
      Resource URL for accessing the Microsoft Dynamics 365 instance
    - **Consumer Key**  
      OAuth consumer key registered with the service provider
    - **Consumer Secret**  
      OAuth consumer secret registered with the service provider
    - **Listener Config**  
      A reference to the listener that catches the access token callback endpoint
    - **Callback Path**  
      The path of the access token callback endpoint
    - **Authorize Path**  
      The path of the local HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance

      The following image shows an example OAuth Connection configuration using property placeholder
      values:

      ![Global element configuration for OAuth Connection](_images/dynamics-oauth-connection.png)

      For the **Authorization url** and **Access token url**, replace the variables
      `<authorization-url>` and `<access-token>` with your own authorization and access URL tokens.

2.  Call the `/authorize` path by pasting it into a web page.
3.  On the returned web page, perform the authorization steps by providing your credentials, and so
    on.

After you complete the proper authorization steps, the web page is automatically redirected to the
path you configured inside the **Callback Path** field, for example, `/oauth2callback`. This
redirect contains additional properties, including an access code for the connector to use.

Once the app is authorized, the connector refreshes the token automatically. However, in the event
of a restart, you must call the `/authorize` path again and perform the authorization steps.

### OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials Authentication

With OAuth 2.0 client credentials authentication, clients authenticate themselves by using a client
ID and client secret.

To configure OAuth 2.0 client credentials authentication, enter the following required information
on the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen:

- **Endpoint**

  Location where to send the requests

- **Client id**

  The OAuth client ID, as registered with the service provider

- **Client secret**

  The OAuth client secret, as registered with the service provider

The following image shows an example OAuth client credentials configuration using property
placeholder values:

![Global element configuration for OAuth 2.0 client credentials](_images/dynamics-365-oauth-client-credentials.png)

### OAuth 2.0 Username and Password Authentication

With OAuth 2.0 username and password authentication, the app makes a POST request that includes a
username and password. If the credentials are valid, the server returns an access token.

To configure OAuth 2.0 username and password authentication, enter the following information on the
**General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen:

- **Username**  
  User name used to initialize the session.
- **Password**  
  Password used to authenticate against the proxy
- **Resource**  
  Application ID URI of the web APIâs secured resource
- **Client ID**  
  Application ID assigned to your app when you registered it with Azure AD. You can find this in the
  Azure Portal:
  1.  Go to the Azure portal.
  2.  Click **Active Directory**, and select the directory.
  3.  Click the application, and then click **Configure**.

- **Client Secret**  
  App secret that you created in the app registration portal for your app

  The app secret should not be used in a native app, because client secrets cannot be reliably
  stored on devices. It is required for web apps and web APIs, which have the ability to store the
  client secret securely on the server side.

- **Token Request Endpoint**  
  URL of the OAuth token request server

The following image shows an example OAuth username and password configuration using property
placeholder values:

![Global element configuration for OAuth 2.0 username and password](_images/dynamics-365-oauth-username-password.png)

## Run a Flow

1.  In Package Explorer, right-click your projectâs name and click **Run As** > **Mule
    Application**.
2.  Check the console to see when the application starts. You should see messages such as these if
    no errors occur:

```text
************************************************************
INFO  2019-10-14 22:12:42,003 [main] org.mule.module.launcher.DeploymentDirectoryWatcher:
++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
+ Mule is up and kicking (every 5000ms)                    +
++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
INFO  2019-10-14 22:12:42,006 [main] org.mule.module.launcher.StartupSummaryDeploymentListener:
**********************************************************
*  - - + DOMAIN + - -               * - - + STATUS + - - *
**********************************************************
* default                           * DEPLOYED           *
**********************************************************

************************************************************************
* - - + APPLICATION + - -   * - - + DOMAIN + - -  * - - + STATUS + - - *
************************************************************************
* myapp                     * default             * DEPLOYED           *
************************************************************************
```

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the output is visible in the Anypoint Studio
  console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log is visible in your OS
  console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`.

## Next Step

After you configure a global element and connection information, see the
[Examples](microsoft-dynamics-365-connector-examples) topic for more configuration information.

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Troubleshooting Microsoft Dynamics 365 Connector 2.6

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To troubleshoot Anypoint Connector for Microsoft Dynamics 365 (Microsoft Dynamics 365 Connector),
become familiar with the information about interpreting commonly thrown exception messages.

## Enable Verbose Exception Logging

Enable verbose exception logs to show a complete stack trace of the error instead of the default
truncated output:

1.  In Studio, right-click on the project and select **Run > Run Configurations**.
2.  Go to the **Arguments** tab and append the arguments in the **VM arguments** section to add the
    `mule.verbose.exceptions` property. For example:

    `-XX:PermSize=128M -XX:MaxPermSize=256M -Dmule.verbose.exceptions=true`

## Enable Verbose Logging

To get a better understanding of why an applicationâs interaction with Microsoft Dynamics 365
Connector is failing, temporarily enable verbose logging for the connector.

Remember to always disable enhanced verbosity after troubleshooting, because it can affect your Mule
applicationâs performance.

To enable verbose logging in the configuration file:

1.  Access Anypoint Studio and navigate to the **Package Explorer** view.
2.  Open your applicationâs project name.
3.  Open the `src/main/resources` folder.
4.  Open the `log4j2.xml` file inside the folder.
5.  Add an `<AsyncLogger>` tag inside the `<Loggers>` tag:

    ```xml
    			<Loggers>
    				...
    				<AsyncLogger name="org.mule.extension.microsoftdynamics365" level="DEBUG"/>
    				...
    			</Loggers>
    ```

6.  Save your application changes.
7.  Click the project name in **Package Explorer** and then click **Run** > **Run As** > **Mule
    Application**.

## Understand Commonly Thrown Exceptions

Here is a list of commonly thrown exception messages and how to interpret them:

- DYNAMICS:CONNECTIVITY

  Either a connection could not be established or there is a connectivity error.

- DYNAMICS:INVALID_PAGE_SIZE

  The page size is invalid, for example, if the fetch size value exceeds 5000.

- DYNAMICS:NOT_FOUND

  The server could not find the requested resource.

- DYNAMICS:OPERATION_FAILED

  The operation fails, for example, if the protocol or entity attributes are invalid or if
  invocation fails.

- DYNAMICS:PARSE_ERROR

  There is an error when parsing a JSON entity, for example, the entity ID is invalid or the
  encoding for the provided JSON is invalid.

- DYNAMICS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

  The consumer is not subscribed to any topics, and no manually assigned partitions are set to
  consume from.

- DYNAMICS:TIMEOUT

  A specific request takes longer than the configuration timeout values.

- DYNAMICS:UNAUTHORIZED

  There is an unauthorized request.

- DYNAMICS:UNKNOWN

  There is an unknown or an unexpected error.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Microsoft Dynamics 365 Connector Reference](microsoft-dynamics-365-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [Known Issues](https://help.salesforce.com/s/issues)
---

# Microsoft Dynamics 365 Connector 2.6 XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

If you manually code a Mule app in XML either from the Anypoint Studio XML editor or from a text
editor, you can get access to the Microsoft Dynamics 365 Connector in your app by adding reference
statements to your XML Mule flow and in the Apache Maven pom.xml file.

> [!NOTE] You can avoid the manual process described in this topic by using Studio. When you install
> Microsoft Dynamics 365 Connector in Anypoint Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code
> with the Microsoft Dynamics 365 Connector namespace and schema location, and adds a dependency to
> the pom.xml file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `mule` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/microsoft-dynamics-365
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/microsoft-dynamics-365/current/mule-microsoft-dynamics-365.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the XML:

```xml
<mule xmlns:$2="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/$2"
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/$2
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/$2/current/mule-$2.xsd">
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app. When you
code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your pom.xml file to enable access to the
connector.

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-microsoft-dynamics365-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `x.x.x` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information, access the connector in
[Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/) and click **Dependency Snippets**.

## Next Step

After completing your namespace and `pom.xml` file, you can try the
[Examples](microsoft-dynamics-365-connector-examples).

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
